one_liner: AMZN bouncing 2.5% on massive 4.69x volume — a Selling Climax absorption signal — but DCF shows 76% downside to $56 fair value; the capex-cycle FCF distortion makes this a 'watch not trade' situation.

news_catalyst.headline: Amazon Showing Selling Climax Signal on 4.69x Volume — Absorption or Distribution?
news_catalyst.summary: AMZN is up 2.5% today on 4.69x average volume — as discussed earlier this week, this pattern (large volume, small price change) is a sign that sellers were met by aggressive buyers (absorption). A HOLD signal is active with entry $228-$235, stop $216, target $257. However, the DCF analysis shows a base intrinsic value of only $56 due to Amazon's massive capex program depressing near-term free cash flow — the same Amazon capex-cycle distortion the backend handles separately. The $56 DCF figure should be treated as distorted by AWS/AI infrastructure investment, not as the true fair value.

technical.summary: AMZN shows the exact absorption pattern discussed this week: 4.69x volume with only 2.5% price change — massive supply being absorbed by buyers at this level. RSI at 35.2 is approaching oversold territory. Bearish MACD persists. Watch for a secondary test of the recent low on lower volume — if the stock holds above $209 support on drying volume, the absorption thesis is confirmed and $257 target becomes viable.

valuation.summary: DCF shows $56 base case — but this is heavily distorted by Amazon's capex-cycle FCF depression from massive AWS/AI infrastructure investment. This DCF figure is not representative of Amazon's true earnings power. Treat valuation as 'not meaningful at this time' rather than 76% downside.

key_strengths: AWS #1 cloud provider with 30%+ market share
Prime ecosystem creates structural revenue moat
key_concerns: Massive capex distorts near-term FCF and DCF analysis
Retail margin pressure from logistics investment
fundamentals.summary: Grade B fundamentals — the business is fundamentally sound but FCF-based analysis is distorted by the AI infrastructure buildout cycle. AWS and advertising together justify a much higher intrinsic value than the $56 DCF suggests.

risks: 4.69x volume absorption signal is promising but needs secondary test to confirm
Bearish MACD — trend not reversed yet
DCF distorted by capex cycle — difficult to value precisely
If support at $209 breaks, next level is much lower
